name: Birmingham (United Kingdom)
description: A field divided per pale indented — blue at the hoist, red at the fly, separated by a gold zig-zag line — charged with a gold bull's head at the centre.
history: Adopted on 23 July 2015 following a public competition. The blue-and-gold indented division derives from the medieval arms of the de Birmingham family, lords of the manor; the bull's head recalls the Bull Ring, the city's historic market.

features.role: field divided per pale indented — blue at the hoist, red at the fly, separated by a gold zig-zag (indented) line
features.symbolism: from the arms of the de Birmingham family (per pale indented or and gules)
the Bull Ring, Birmingham's historic market
